FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Prediction markets hitting records amid regulatory momentum, while Macquarie makes bullish call on Chinese AI chips.

bullets.text: Prediction market platforms (Kalshi, Polymarket, Rothera) smashed record volumes in June—Rothera alone did $2B—signaling mainstreaming of retail derivatives trading and potential regulatory tailwind.
Macquarie initiates coverage on five Chinese AI chip makers with buy ratings, positioning itself as the lead voice on a sector facing US sanctions risk but potential domestic upside.

Markets & Macro.lede: Tech valuations repricing and momentum trades at risk, but softer jobs data opens bond opportunity as market breadth narrows beneath surface gains.

bullets.text: Tech bubble deflating: FT warns deteriorating valuations signal broader repricing; old-fashioned valuation discipline will outperform in downturn ahead.
Momentum unwind risk in July: Record positioning + thin summer liquidity creates elevated risk of violent reversal in historically weak month for momentum trades.
June jobs miss (217k payrolls) signals lower inflation pressure and potential Fed rate-cut catalyst, bullish for bonds beneath equity rally masking narrowing breadth.

Newsletters.lede: Index fund strategy under pressure as advisors seek alternatives; psilocybin treatments gain policy momentum but face regulatory hurdles.

bullets.text: Advisors dumping broad index funds due to performance drag from low-quality/distressed holdings diluting mega-cap gains.
Psilocybin treatments winning White House backing for treatment-resistant depression, but clinical validation and regulatory path remain unclear—biotech upside with execution risk.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Four opinion pieces on American exceptionalism and AI's productivity gains, plus a construction roundup touching chip exports and power grids.

bullets.text: Noah Smith: U.S. economic dominance over 250 years correlates with—and arguably caused—broad global human flourishing, not coincidence.
Tyler Cowen: AI collapses the cost of intellectual exploration (hours vs. weeks), lowering the bar for which ideas get developed and potentially flooding the market with half-baked concepts.
Construction Physics notes: AI chip export controls and Japan's dual-frequency grid signal infrastructure constraints; Meta's AI compute strategy underscores the race for computational edge.

all.lede: Tech valuations cracking, labor market softening, and AI reshaping intellectual productivity—signals of market regime change beneath surface calm.

bullets.text: Tech bubble deflating: FT argues old-fashioned valuation discipline will outperform as deteriorating multiples signal broader market repricing ahead.
June jobs miss (217k vs. May's 272k) and softer wage growth signal lower inflation pressure—potential catalyst for Fed rate cuts and bond rally.
Equity gains masking structural shift: AI-linked trades concentrating returns while breadth narrows and bond yields reset lower—regime change afoot.
